india_origin_audit.useful_lead: MFC-for-semiconductor market-size estimate (USD 1.05B in 2025 -> USD 2B by 2035, 6.5% CAGR) and vendor share list (HORIBA 17%, MKS 14%, Fujikin, Sevenstar, Hitachi Metals, Pivotal Systems, Bronkhorst, Brooks, Sensirion) is worth independently re-sourcing from a non-Indian consultancy (e.g., a competing Fortune Business Insights or Yole report) before use in market sizing.
india_origin_audit.notes: Global Growth Insights' official contact page lists its address as Office No.-B, 2nd Floor, Icon Tower, Baner-Mhalunge Road, Baner, Pune 411045, Maharashtra, India -- an India-registered market-research consultancy despite the global-sounding brand name, matching the exact pattern flagged in the audit directive (cf. DataIntelo/Persistence Market Research). Per binding source-origin rule, downgraded to discovery_only; the record was already T3/context-only per SOURCE_STANDARDS so this does not remove any load-bearing TAM claim, but the record must not be cited as an accepted source.

title: Two major demand drivers boost RF power-supply market growth; domestic manufacturers gaining strength
authors_or_org: Yicai (Diyi Caijing) financial media
claim_supported: China's semiconductor RF power-supply market was valued at approximately US$0.97 billion in 2022 (13.31% of the global market), with a low domestic-localization rate and the global market dominated by two US manufacturers (MKS, Advanced Energy) plus Japanese/German competitors. Hengyunchang has a strategic-cooperation agreement with Tuojing Technology, with some RF-power products passing verification/entering mass production. Injet Electric has reportedly achieved 80-90% domestic substitution on 6-inch/8-inch fab lines over five years, targeting 50% coverage on 12-inch lines within five years. Shanghai Yianen Semiconductor, which began in RF-generator repair services, claims 'nearly 95% of wafer fabs' as customers.
locator: Directly fetched yicai.com/news/102170278.html this session.
notes: CORRECTION: the actually-fetched article content does not support the scout's original claim of a '<12% localization rate' figure (that specific figure could not be reproduced this session and is dropped); the article's real claims (market size, share, and named-company substitution rates) are used instead. This materially changes and strengthens the record: Injet Electric's claimed 80-90% domestic substitution on legacy nodes is a notable, more specific demand data point than the dropped figure.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Wonik IPS -- Business Overview
authors_or_org: Wonik IPS Co., Ltd. (Korea, KOSDAQ: 240810)
claim_supported: Wonik IPS states it develops advanced circuit-fabrication process equipment, primarily thin-film deposition equipment; achieved mass production of 3D NAND Flash mold-process equipment in 2014 and entered the 10nm-class High-K DRAM market in 2018. Over 60% of Wonik IPS revenue comes from Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix.
